prompt:
You are a PROOF-FILLER. Replace the `sorry` placeholders for the listed declarations with complete, type-correct Lean proofs. Do NOT change statement signatures; do NOT weaken hypotheses.

Lean files live under: /<repo-root>/CausalSmith/CausalSmith/Substrate/BernsteinSzegoTrig
Target declarations: CausalSmith.Substrate.BernsteinSzegoTrig.IsTrigPolyLE.card_zeros_le

## Instructions
Work in the Lean 4 + Mathlib project at /<repo-root>/CausalSmith. Prove ONE hard declaration by replacing its `sorry` in `CausalSmith/Substrate/BernsteinSzegoTrig/TrigPoly.lean`. This is the load-bearing lemma of a Szegő-inequality substrate; treat it as a real project. Do NOT change the statement, add axioms, `sorry`, or weaken hypotheses. Verify with `lake build CausalSmith.Substrate.BernsteinSzegoTrig.TrigPoly` (only `sorry` warnings from OTHER decls in that file, no errors). Use lean-lsp tools (`lean_goal`, `lean_loogle`, `lean_leansearch`, `lean_hover_info`) heavily to find exact Mathlib names.

Predicate: `IsTrigPolyLE n f := ∃ a b : ℕ → ℝ, ∀ t, f t = ∑ k ∈ Finset.range (n+1), (a k * Real.cos ((k:ℝ)*t) + b k * Real.sin ((k:ℝ)*t))`.

Target: `IsTrigPolyLE.card_zeros_le {n} {f} (hf : IsTrigPolyLE n f) (hne : ∃ t, f t ≠ 0) {S : Finset ℝ} (hS : ↑S ⊆ Set.Ico 0 (2*Real.pi)) (hzero : ∀ t ∈ S, f t = 0) : S.card ≤ 2*n`. I.e. a nonzero real trig polynomial of degree ≤ n has at most 2n zeros in one period.

Proof route (z = e^{it}). Obtain `a b` from `hf`. On the unit circle `z = Complex.exp (I t)`, we have `Real.cos(k t) = ((z^k + z^{-k})/2).re`… but cleaner: work entirely in ℂ. Define the complex polynomial `P : Polynomial ℂ` of degree ≤ 2n by
`P = ∑ k ∈ Finset.range (n+1), (Complex.ofReal (a k)/2 - Complex.ofReal (b k) * Complex.I/2) • X^(n+k)  +  (Complex.ofReal (a k)/2 + Complex.ofReal (b k)*Complex.I/2) • X^(n-k)`
(i.e. build it via `Finset.sum` of `Polynomial.C _ * Polynomial.X ^ _`). KEY IDENTITY: for every real t, with `z := Complex.exp (Complex.I * t)`,
`P.eval z = z^n * ((f t : ℝ) : ℂ)`.
This follows from `cos(kt) = (e^{ikt}+e^{-ikt})/2`, `sin(kt) = (e^{ikt}-e^{-ikt})/(2i)` (Mathlib: `Complex.cos`, `Complex.sin`, `Complex.exp_mul_I`, `Real.cos`/`Complex.ofReal_cos` bridging `Complex.cos (t:ℂ) = (Real.cos t : ℂ)`; look up `Complex.ofReal_cos`, `Complex.exp_ofReal_mul_I_re`, `Complex.cos_eq`, `Complex.sin_eq`). Note `z^k = exp(I k t)`, `z^{-k} = exp(-I k t)` and `z ≠ 0` (`Complex.exp_ne_zero`).

Consequences:
- `P ≠ 0`: if `P = 0` then `P.eval z = 0` for all t, so `z^n * (f t) = 0`, and `z^n ≠ 0`, giving `f t = 0` for all t, contradicting `hne`. (`natDegree ≤ 2n` also gives `P.eval` a genuine poly; but nonzeroness is what matters for roots.)
- For each `t ∈ S`, `f t = 0` so `P.eval (exp (I t)) = 0`, i.e. `exp (I t)` is a root of `P`.
- The map `t ↦ Complex.exp (Complex.I * t)` is INJECTIVE on `[0, 2π)` (distinct arguments in a length-2π window give distinct points on the circle). Prove via: if `exp(I s)=exp(I t)` with `s,t ∈ [0,2π)` then `s = t` — use `Complex.exp_eq_exp_iff_exists_int` / `Complex.exp_eq_one_iff`, periodicity `2π`, and the window bound. Search `lean_leansearch "complex exponential injective on interval"`.
- Therefore `S.image (fun t => Complex.exp (Complex.I * t))` has card `= S.card` (injective on S ⊆ [0,2π)) and is ⊆ the finset of roots of `P`. Distinct roots of a nonzero polynomial number ≤ its degree: use `Polynomial.card_roots_le_degree` / `Polynomial.roots` with `Multiset.toFinset`, or `Polynomial.setOf_isRoot_finite`; the count of distinct roots ≤ `P.natDegree ≤ 2n`.
- Bound `P.natDegree ≤ 2*n`: every monomial has exponent `n+k ≤ 2n` or `n-k ≤ n ≤ 2n` for `k ≤ n`; use `Polynomial.natDegree_sum_le`, `Polynomial.natDegree_C_mul_X_pow_le` etc.

Assemble: `S.card = (S.image e).card ≤ P.roots.toFinset.card ≤ P.natDegree ≤ 2n`.

This is long. Introduce private helper lemmas ABOVE the target in the same file as needed (the eval identity, injectivity, natDegree bound), all non-`sorry`. Search for exact lemma names before using them; verify each with `lean_hover_info`. If you get genuinely stuck on ONE named sub-lemma after real effort, leave THAT sub-lemma the only `sorry`, finish everything else, and report precisely which sub-lemma blocked you and why. Report the final `lake build` tail.

Use the lean-lsp MCP tools to check your proof (diagnostics/goal). When done, output a SHORT plain-text report: which decls you closed, which remain, and any blocker. No JSON required.
